description: Completes the data layer architecture. Wires Pusher (or compatible service) for real-time events, connects invalidation strategy to the query key registry, and establishes the per-feature realtime.ts pattern. Also finalises the query config with all stale time tiers applied across existing queries. This MVP delivers infrastructure only — no new screens.

realtime_architecture.service: Pusher Channels (or Ably/Soketi as drop-in replacements)

pattern.description: Each feature owns its realtime.ts file. It subscribes to Pusher channels and invalidates the relevant TanStack Query keys. The Pusher client is a singleton in src/lib/realtime/pusher.ts.

files.src/lib/realtime/pusher.ts: Singleton Pusher client. Initialized once. Exported as pusher.
files.src/features/<feature>/realtime.ts: Per-feature hook. Subscribes on mount, unsubscribes on unmount. Calls queryClient.invalidateQueries with keys from src/lib/query/keys.ts.

connection_lifecycle: Pusher client initialized in src/lib/realtime/pusher.ts on import — not on component mount
useRealtimeConnection hook in root layout: connects on mount, disconnects on unmount
On reconnect: invalidate all active queries — queryClient.invalidateQueries()
Connection state logged via logger.info on connect/disconnect/error

auth.description: If using private/presence channels, Pusher needs an auth endpoint on your backend.
auth.note: Public channels require no auth — use for template default. Private channels wired per-app.

query_architecture_completion.description: Apply stale time tiers from src/lib/query/config.ts to all existing and future queries. Document the decision for each.

query_assignments.reason: Session data changes only on login/logout/profile update — 10min cache is safe

rules: Every useQuery hook must reference a key from src/lib/query/keys.ts — no inline string arrays
Every useQuery hook must set staleTime from src/lib/query/config.ts STALE constants — no hardcoded ms values
Real-time data: staleTime=STALE.realtime + Pusher invalidation — never poll
Static/config data: staleTime=STALE.static, fetched once per session
Form screens: set enabled:false or staleTime=STALE.static to prevent background refetch mid-fill

feature_realtime_pattern.description: Template for any feature that needs real-time. Copy this pattern per feature.

example_code.description: useNotificationsRealtime() subscribes to user-specific Pusher channel and invalidates notification queries on events

query_keys_expansion.description: Expand src/lib/query/keys.ts with all keys needed by MVP 1-5. Future features add their own keys here.

files_to_create.description: Singleton Pusher client. Reads EXPO_PUBLIC_PUSHER_KEY and EXPO_PUBLIC_PUSHER_CLUSTER from env. Exports pusher instance.
Exports pusher and useRealtimeConnection. useRealtimeConnection manages connect/disconnect on AppState changes and logs connection events.

changes: Call useRealtimeConnection() inside RootLayout to manage Pusher lifecycle
Add refetchOnReconnect: true to defaultOptions.queries — TanStack Query resumes on reconnect automatically

data_flow: 1. App boots — useRealtimeConnection initializes Pusher connection
2. User authenticates — feature realtime hooks mount and subscribe to user-specific channels
3. Backend emits Pusher event on data change
4. Feature realtime.ts receives event, calls queryClient.invalidateQueries with specific key
5. TanStack Query refetches the stale query in background
6. UI updates automatically via useQuery subscriber
7. App backgrounds — Pusher disconnects, TanStack Query pauses background refetch
8. App foregrounds — Pusher reconnects, queryClient.invalidateQueries() triggers full refresh

acceptance_criteria.then: Pusher connection is established. Connection state logged as info.

acceptance_criteria.given: A feature component mounts with useNotificationsRealtime()
acceptance_criteria.when: Backend emits notification.created on the user's channel
acceptance_criteria.then: notifications query is invalidated and UI updates without manual refresh

acceptance_criteria.given: App returns to foreground after background
acceptance_criteria.then: Pusher reconnects and queryClient.invalidateQueries() triggers a full refresh of active queries

acceptance_criteria.given: A new feature query is added to the codebase
acceptance_criteria.then: Query key is in src/lib/query/keys.ts and staleTime references STALE constants — no inline values

edge_cases: Pusher connection may fail on first connect (bad key, network) — log error, do not crash app. Real-time is enhancement, not critical path.
On reconnect, invalidateQueries() is broad — for high-frequency apps, scope it to only active query keys using queryClient.getQueryCache().getAll()
pusher-js uses XMLHttpRequest which may be blocked by Android cleartext policy on dev — ensure dev backend uses HTTPS or configure network_security_config.xml
Multiple feature realtime hooks subscribing to the same channel is fine — Pusher deduplicates channel subscriptions
If Pusher is not configured (no env vars), useRealtimeConnection should silently no-op — never throw on missing config
out_of_scope: Pusher presence channels — template uses public/private channels only
WebSocket fallback if Pusher is unavailable — use Pusher's built-in transport fallback
tRPC subscriptions — Pusher is the real-time layer, tRPC handles request/response only
Per-feature realtime implementations beyond the template pattern — apps add their own
Offline queue (sending mutations while offline and replaying on reconnect) — app-level concern
